jobs.title: Junior Ops HEG
jobs.description:
Your team

 Plongez au cœur de l’univers bancaire durant vos 4 ans de formation à la Haute école de gestion (HEG).

 Vous aurez l’opportunité de découvrir plusieurs services des opérations bancaires à travers des rotations enrichissantes. En collaborant étroitement avec nos équipes, vous participerez activement aux opérations quotidiennes de la Banque, tout en veillant à l’efficacité et à la conformité des processus.

 Ce programme unique vous permettra de développer vos compétences et d’acquérir une connaissance approfondie des métiers de la banque privée.

 Your role

 

• Contribuer activement aux projets et missions confiés par les responsables des différents services lors de vos rotations.

• Vous adapter rapidement à un environnement professionnel exigeant et dynamique, au cœur de la banque privée.

• Développer vos compétences techniques et relationnelles en collaborant avec des experts du secteur bancaire.

 

 Your profile

 Vous souhaitez intégrer la Haute Ecole de Gestion en emploi à 60% pour allier vos études à une expérience professionnelle enrichissante.

 Avec de bons résultats scolaires et témoignant d’un réel intérêt pour le domaine bancaire, vous êtes motivé/e à développer vos compétences et à mettre vos connaissances en pratique dans un environnement dynamique et stimulant.

 Organisé/e, rigoureux/se, curieux/se et persévérant/e, vous appréciez le travail en équipe et communiquez avec aisance. Vous maîtrisez parfaitement le français, disposez de bonnes bases en anglais, et la connaissance d’une autre langue est un atout. A l’aise avec les outils informatiques courants MS Office, vous êtes domicilié/e en Suisse et prêt/e à relever ce défi. Envoyez-nous dès maintenant votre dossier complet pour rejoindre notre programme!

 Note

 We will not accept any CVs via agencies

 Diversity & Inclusion

 Pictet is an equal opportunity employer and is committed to creating a diverse environment. We respect all individuals and seek their inclusion in the workplace.

jobs.description:
Your team

 

 The Corporate Functions division encompasses the Group’s non-banking activities and comprises over 600 employees. Staffed by specialists with deep expertise across all business lines, the central functions are organised into various group departments: Communication, Compliance, Finance, Human Resources, Information Security, Internal Audit, Legal, Corporate Office, Risk and Taxation.

 

 The Financial Institutions Management (FIM) team, part of the Finance Department, is the Group's centre of expertise for managing relationships with financial institutions. As a key member of this team, you will help maintain and enhance the Group's vital relationships with our financial counterparties.

 Your role

 

 We are seeking a proactive and analytical professional to take ownership of optimising data management, streamlining workflows, and driving process automation across the team's activities. You will lead the transformation and implementation of these initiatives to ensure continuous improvement and innovation.

 
You will be responsible for the following tasks:

 
Process improvement and automation

 • Analyse and map current processes, systems and tools across the team's activities.
• Identify inefficiencies and take the initiative to propose and implement improvements.
• Take ownership of automation solutions and drive the digital transformation of our workflows, leveraging advanced analytics and the latest technologies.
• Reduce reliance on manual processes and email-based requests by introducing robust, scalable solutions.

 Data Management and Quality

 • Maintain and enhance financial counterparty master data (codification, static data).
• Lead efforts to build and manage a centralised “golden source” of data, ensuring high data quality and consistency.
• Implement and monitor automated data quality controls, ensuring data reconciliation and consistency across systems.

 Reporting and Monitoring

 • Prepare impactful dashboards reports, and presentations for internal teams and senior management, using state-of-the-art data visualisation tools.
• Participate in projects that impact the team's activities, driving innovation, efficiency and the adoption of new data science methodologies.

 Your profile

 

 • Master’s degree or equivalent.
• 3 to 5 years of experience and keen interest in banking, or previous experience in the banking industry. 
• Experience in KYC/AML would be a plus.
• Strong analytical and problem-solving mindset.
• Proactive, solution-oriented and able to take initiative, structured, with strong attention to detail.
• Committed, results-driven and accountable.
• Strong team spirit with the ability to collaborate across teams and business lines.
• Keen interest in process improvement, automation and data visualisation, leveraging technology.
• Experience in SQL and Python.
• Experience with Business Intelligence Tools (Alteryx, Power BI, Tableau) and in Gen AI project implementation.
• Familiarity with data management concepts (data quality/governance).
• Fluent in French and English; other languages are a plus.
• Must be resident in Switzerland.

 Note

 We will not accept any CVs via agencies.

 Ref. : D&PAFC/CC/AK

 Diversity & Inclusion

 Pictet is an equal opportunity employer and is committed to creating a diverse environment. We respect all individuals and seek their inclusion in the workplace.

jobs.description:
Your team

 The Pictet Tech division specialises in designing and integrating cutting-edge software applications, including advanced portfolio management systems, sophisticated trading platforms and comprehensive banking and corporate solutions. As a key contributor to the Group’s strategic advancements, the Pictet Tech division plays a vital role in driving transformative innovations that enhance our services and deliver exceptional value to our clients.
As a Cyber Risk & Compliance Manager within Pictet Tech, you will be part of a dynamic Tech & Cyber Risk Management department based in Geneva. You will be responsible for maintaining and enhancing the Internal Control System for cybersecurity processes for the Pictet Tech division. In this pivotal role, you will assess the design and performance of cybersecurity controls and conduct risk analyses to evaluate the impact of cyber threats. Your mission will also include overseeing the development and alignment of cybersecurity documentation and practices with regulatory requirements.

 Your role

 

• Maintain the Internal Control System in relation to cybersecurity processes at the Tech level, in order to manage cyber risk in an effective manner.

• Assess the design and performance of 1st level cybersecurity controls carried out by Tech teams, and support the annual risk & control self assessments.

• Perform risk analyses and contribute to assessing the impact of emerging cyber threats on Pictet’s Tech landscape.

• Oversee the development, maintenance and review of cybersecurity documentation, including policies, standards and procedures. Ensure these are aligned with regulatory requirements.

• Serve as the principal point of contact for regulatory reviews and external audits related to cybersecurity.

• Perform regular 2nd level controls (e.g. shadow cloud, access bypass, etc.).

• Play an active role within the Risk & Compliance team, contributing to KPI & KRI documentation, providing cybersecurity support to IT projects and leading follow-up on improvement actions resulting from incidents, assessments or audits.

 

 Your profile

 

• Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Information Security, or a related field.

• 3-5 years of experience in cybersecurity risk management, consulting or audit, with a strong foundation in technical concepts and practices.

• Proven expertise in Identity and Access Management and Privileged Access Management solutions, with successful track records in implementation, monitoring, and optimisation.

• Strong grasp of cyber hygiene best practices, including experience with CIS baseline controls across endpoints, databases and servers. Ability to design and enforce IT infrastructure security policies.

• Familiarity with financial sector cyber related regulations (FINMA, BaFin, MAS, HKMA, DORA, etc).

• Relevant professional certifications (e.g., CISSP, CISM or equivalent) is a plus.

• Self-driven, proactive and solution-oriented mindset with excellent problem-solving skills.

• Fluency in both French and English, with strong communication and interpersonal skills.

• Residency in Switzerland or willingness to relocate.

 
 Ref. CR&CM/CRO/RB

 Note

 We will not accept any CVs via agencies

 Pictet is an equal opportunity employer and is committed to creating a diverse environment. We respect all individuals and seek their inclusion in the workplace.

jobs.description:
業務概要

 オルタナティブ投資の中でも、プライベート資産（プライベートエクイティ、プライベートデット、不動産）およびこれらの低流動性資産を組み入れるマルチアセット戦略の開発を担当するプロダクトスペシャリストとして、受託者責任（fiduciary）および説明責任（accountability）を果たしつつ、ビジネスの拡大に貢献していただきます。

 金融市場およびプライベート資産に関する専門知識を活かし、グローバル運用部門や営業部門など関連部署と連携しながら新規案件の獲得や既存案件の管理を行います。さらに、商品開発・マーケティング・バックオフィス等の社内ステークホルダーと協働し、クライアントニーズに即した商品組成やソリューション構築、マルチアセット戦略の開発に積極的に関与していただきます。

 

 主な職務内容

 

• 顧客への運用ソリューションの提案

• 担当プロダクトの顧客向け説明およびビジネスデベロップメント活動（営業部門との協働）

• プライベート資産等の低流動性資産を組み入れるマルチアセット戦略の企画・開発

• 担当プロダクトの運用状況の把握、運用報告会や運用報告書等での説明

• RFPや顧客からの質問への対応（RFPチームと協働）

• 担当プロダクトに関する戦略、運用モニタリングおよびデューデリジェンス

• プロダクトの品質維持・管理

• その他関連業務

 
 ※グローバルの運用チーム、プロダクト・スペシャリスト部門、商品本部内の他部門（商品開発、RFP、運用データ分析等）、営業部門、その他関連部門と連携し、上記業務を遂行していただきます。

 望ましい経験・最低経験年数

 

• 金融業界（運用会社、証券会社等）における5年超の経験

 
 

• プライベート資産（プライベートエクイティ、プライベートデット、不動産）に関連する知識、業務経験

 
 

 英語： ビジネス・レベル、 日本語： ネイティブレベル

 We will not accept any CVs via agencies

 Pictet is an equal opportunity employer and is committed to creating a diverse environment. We respect all individuals and seek their inclusion in the workplace.

jobs.title: Client Relationship Officer
jobs.description:
Votre équipe

 Le Groupe Pictet, fondé en 1805, possède son siège à Genève en Suisse. Le Groupe est établi dans le monde entier (31 bureaux) et emploie actuellement plus de 5000 personnes. La division Pictet Wealth Management offre depuis plus de 200 ans à ses clients une gamme étendue de services bancaires et une expertise mondiale en matière d'investissement.

 

 En tant que Client Relationship Officer vous serez rattaché/e au service Marché – France de la division Pictet Wealth Management à Paris. Vous assisterez plusieurs banquiers dans tous les aspects de la gestion administrative et opérationnelle des comptes clients d’une part, et assurerez diverses tâches de support à l’activité de planification financière d’autre part.

 Votre rôle

 

• Être l’interlocuteur/trice de référence pour toutes les demandes opérationnelles des banquiers et des clients, en assurant un service de qualité et une communication proactive.

• Faciliter toutes les étapes du processus d’ouverture de compte, en veillant au respect des prescriptions réglementaires locales et à la complétude des documents requis.

• Gérer et suivre les opérations courantes : codifications de compte, tarification, renouvellement des limites de crédit, opérations de paiement et de transfert, appels de vérification, etc.

• Assurer un suivi rigoureux de la documentation (suspens, remédiation, listes Risk & Compliance) et, dans ce cadre, contacter les clients en coordination avec les banquiers responsables des relations concernées.

• Générer, personnaliser et transmettre les reportings financiers et administratifs aux clients et partenaires ; récupérer les situations des contrats et demander les reportings spécifiques aux équipes internes.

• Suivre les soldes débiteurs : monitorer les comptes, contacter les clients pour définir un plan d’action, effectuer les transferts ou ventes d’actifs nécessaires, et coordonner avec les équipes concernées.

• Traiter les opérations spécifiques liées à l’assurance vie et à la gestion de contrats : réception et gestion des mandats de gestion, arbitrages, instructions de désinvestissement, résiliations, changements de contraintes, de tarification, etc.

• Assurer la prise en charge des appels téléphoniques et le soutien au sein de l’équipe.

• Contribuer à la collecte et à la mise à jour des informations KYC dans les systèmes internes.

• Travailler en étroite collaboration avec les équipes internes pour garantir la fluidité des opérations.

 

 Votre profil

 

• Bachelor ou formation jugée équivalente.

• 5 ans d’expérience minimum en tant que Client Relationship Officer (ou poste équivalent) dans une banque privée, idéalement au sein d’un établissement majeur du secteur.

• Aptitude à travailler avec plusieurs banquiers et sur deux activités différentes. 

• À l’aise dans un environnement informatique complexe et évolutif, avec la capacité d’utiliser efficacement de nombreux outils spécifiques à la banque privée.

• Bonne vision transversale des différents départements de la banque privée, ainsi qu’une compréhension des rôles et des interactions entre chaque service.

• Connaissance des dispositifs et prescriptions réglementaires (activités transfrontalières, gestion des risques et compliance, PEP, relations à risques accrus, FATCA, norme commune de déclaration, etc.).

• Le français est votre langue maternelle.

• Très bonne compréhension écrite et orale de l’anglais.

 
 

 Précis/e, méticuleux/se et organisé/e tout en sachant faire preuve de flexibilité et de discrétion, vous êtes doté/e d’une excellente présentation et prenez volontiers des responsabilités.

 Doté/e d’une très bonne capacité d’adaptation et sachant parfaitement gérer les priorités, vous êtes polyvalent/e et travaillez aisément pour deux activités et équipes différentes.

 Vous savez faire preuve d’initiative et travailler de manière autonome, tout en respectant la confidentialité des activités dont vous avez la charge.

 Note

 We will not accept any CVs via agencies

 Diversité & Inclusion

 Pictet est un employeur qui respecte l’égalité des chances et favorise la diversité. Le respect des personnes et l’inclusion au travail sont au cœur de nos préoccupations.

• Être l’interlocuteur/trice de référence pour toutes les demandes opérationnelles des banquiers et des clients, en assurant un service de qualité et une communication proactive.

• Faciliter toutes les étapes du processus d’ouverture de compte, en veillant au respect des prescriptions réglementaires locales et à la complétude des documents requis.

• Gérer et suivre les opérations courantes : codifications de compte, tarification, renouvellement des limites de crédit, opérations de paiement et de transfert, appels de vérification, etc.

• Assurer un suivi rigoureux de la documentation (suspens, remédiation, listes Risk & Compliance) et, dans ce cadre, contacter les clients en coordination avec les banquiers responsables des relations concernées.

• Traiter les opérations spécifiques liées à l’assurance vie et à la gestion de contrats : réception et gestion des mandats de gestion, arbitrages, instructions de désinvestissement, résiliations, changements de contraintes, de tarification, etc.

• Assurer la prise en charge des appels téléphoniques et le soutien au sein de l’équipe.

• Contribuer à la collecte et à la mise à jour des informations KYC dans les systèmes internes.

jobs.description:
Your team

 The Group Corporate Functions division offers Pictet Group clients and employees a wide range of services; it manages the Group’s headquarters in Geneva as well as its subsidiaries, branches and offices in Switzerland and abroad, and ensures their physical security.

 To strengthen the Event Management team, our Geneva office is seeking to hire an Event Manager.

 Your role

 Oversee the design, planning and execution of internal and external events for the Pictet Group, ensuring they run smoothly, align with the company’s strategic objectives and enhance the Group’s image.

 Key responsibilities:

 

• Oversee the entire operational process of events, from defining requirements to execution.

• Coordinate internal and external stakeholders (vendors, internal teams, partners).

• Ensure the quality, consistency and image of the Group at every event.

• Work closely with the marketing and communications teams of the various business lines to design tailored offerings.

• Participate in the development and promotion of the catalogue of internal and external event services.

• Contribute to the opening and operation of the Group’s new event spaces.

 
 Specific tasks:

 

• Prepare event specifications and budgets.

• Select and manage vendors and negotiate rates (venues, caterers, technical aspects, etc.).

• Oversee logistics (food and beverage, relocations, travel, accommodations).

• Maintain communication with teams before, during and after the event.

• Manage unforeseen issues and handle post-event follow-up (review, feedback).

• Ensure compliance with internal procedures and quality standards.

• Propose innovations and continuous improvements in event organisation.

 

 Your profile

 

• Advanced degree in communication, marketing, event management or equivalent.

• Proven experience in event management, ideally in the luxury, banking or institutional sectors (minimum 5 to 8 years).

• Excellent organizational, interpersonal and writing skills.

• Ability to work in a team and rally others around shared projects.

• Diligence, independence, creativity, stress management and a service-oriented mindset.

• Fluency in French and English (spoken and written).

• Proficiency in office software.

• Residence in Switzerland required.

 
 Ref. EM/SBP/AKL

 Note

 We will not accept any CVs via agencies

 Diversity & Inclusion

 Pictet is an equal opportunity employer and is committed to creating a diverse environment. We respect all individuals and seek their inclusion in the workplace.

jobs.description:
Your team

 Operations provide secure and efficient processing of Pictet’s banking transactions and investment funds. The division delivers high-quality operational services to business lines including Wealth Management, Asset Services and Asset Management. 

 

 As Fund Distribution Commissions Specialist , you will be part of the Investment Fund Commissions team and responsible for processing trailer fee commissions for our clients across all the Pictet group entities, while contributing to the continuous improvement of processes through the integration of advanced technical solutions and automation tools.

 Your role

 

• Calculate, reconcile and allocate trailer fee commissions in accordance with contractual agreements and internal policies.

• Maintain accurate records of commission structures and ensure data integrity.

• Prepare regular and ad hoc reports on commission payments, outstanding balances, and related metrics for management and stakeholders.

• Actively contribute to IT-focused projects, optimizing operational workflows through the implementation of automated solutions and advanced technologies.

• Act as the point of contact for queries related to commission payments.

• Ensure activities comply with regulations, internal controls, and audit requirements.

• Support the onboarding of new relationships.

 

 Your profile

 

• Bachelor’s degree in finance, IT or equivalent qualification.

• Experience in banking operations and understanding of investment funds and distribution channels are important assets, but it is not a decisive criterion if your profile demonstrates a strong ability to structure and optimize processes.

• Strong interest in automation and new technologies with the ability to design and maintain automated operational tools and solutions (e.g., Python, SQL, low-code, LLMs, AI agents, etc.) is a valued asset.

• Analytical, problem-solving, and attention-to-detail skills, with the ability to translate business needs into effective technical solutions.

• Proven ability to manage multiple tasks and deadlines in a fast-paced environment.

• Collaborative approach, with a willingness to support colleagues and contribute to a positive team environment.

• Excellent written and verbal communication skills in French and English, with the ability to interact effectively with both internal and external stakeholders.

 

 Note

 FDCS/JLS/MAB/

 

 We will not accept any CVs via agencies

 Diversity & Inclusion

 Pictet is an equal opportunity employer and is committed to creating a diverse environment. We respect all individuals and seek their inclusion in the workplace.

 Operations provide secure and efficient processing of Pictet’s banking transactions and investment funds. The division delivers high-quality operational services to business lines including Wealth Management, Asset Services and Asset Management. 

 

 As Fund Distribution Commissions Specialist , you will be part of the Investment Fund Commissions team and responsible for processing trailer fee commissions for our clients across all the Pictet group entities, while contributing to the continuous improvement of processes through the integration of advanced technical solutions and automation tools.

 Your role

 

• Calculate, reconcile and allocate trailer fee commissions in accordance with contractual agreements and internal policies.

• Maintain accurate records of commission structures and ensure data integrity.

• Prepare regular and ad hoc reports on commission payments, outstanding balances, and related metrics for management and stakeholders.

• Actively contribute to IT-focused projects, optimizing operational workflows through the implementation of automated solutions and advanced technologies.

• Act as the point of contact for queries related to commission payments.

• Ensure activities comply with regulations, internal controls, and audit requirements.

• Support the onboarding of new relationships.

 

 Your profile

 

• Bachelor’s degree in finance, IT or equivalent qualification.

• Experience in banking operations and understanding of investment funds and distribution channels are important assets, but it is not a decisive criterion if your profile demonstrates a strong ability to structure and optimize processes.

• Strong interest in automation and new technologies with the ability to design and maintain automated operational tools and solutions (e.g., Python, SQL, low-code, LLMs, AI agents, etc.) is a valued asset.

• Analytical, problem-solving, and attention-to-detail skills, with the ability to translate business needs into effective technical solutions.

• Proven ability to manage multiple tasks and deadlines in a fast-paced environment.

• Collaborative approach, with a willingness to support colleagues and contribute to a positive team environment.

• Excellent written and verbal communication skills in French and English, with the ability to interact effectively with both internal and external stakeholders.

 

 Note

 FDCS/JLS/MAB/

 

 We will not accept any CVs via agencies

 Diversity & Inclusion

 Pictet is an equal opportunity employer and is committed to creating a diverse environment. We respect all individuals and seek their inclusion in the workplace.

jobs.description:
Your team

 Operations provide secure and efficient processing of Pictet’s banking transactions and investment funds. Our teams ensure all activities follow regulations and manage risks.

 By developing and maintaining a modern, robust infrastructure, we guarantee reliable, high-quality services for Pictet wealth management, asset management and real estate clients.

 

 We are looking for a Private Assets Operations Specialist to join our Funds and Private Assets Services unit. Our department belongs to the Operations division, which provides a broad range of custodian and fund administration services. Based in Geneva, you will be joining a dynamic team that plays a central role in supporting the strategic growth of our Private Assets offering.

 Your role

 

• Execute subscriptions and transfer orders for Private Equity Funds and Private Placements.

• Book the transactions after confirmation.

• Operate the Organized Trading Facility of Interests.

• Resolve issues arising from reconciliations.

• Coordinate the reception of in-kind distributions.

• Prepare customized advice.

• Support and liaise with internal clients on a daily basis.

• Manage risks associated with Private Assets operations.

 

 Your profile

 

• University degree (Bachelor’s or Master's) in Business and/or Economics, or the equivalent in work experience in the finance industry.

• Experience of 1 to 5 years in Private Assets Operations is a plus.

• Good user of Excel and generally at ease with new IT systems.

• Quick learning ability.

• Rigorous, team player and sense of responsibility.

• Solution-oriented and ability to manage complex cases.

• Fluent in French and English with excellent communication skills.

• Resident in Switzerland or willing to relocate.

 

 Note

 PAOS / JLS / LS

 

 We will not accept any CVs via agencies

 Diversity & Inclusion

 Pictet is an equal opportunity employer and is committed to creating a diverse environment. We respect all individuals and seek their inclusion in the workplace.

jobs.description:
Your team

 

 The Corporate Functions division encompasses the Group’s non-banking activities and comprises over 600 employees. Staffed by specialists with deep expertise across all business lines, the central functions are organised into various group departments: Communication, Compliance, Finance, Human Resources, Information Security, Internal Audit, Legal, Corporate Office, Risk and Taxation.

 

 We provide senior management with business and financial insights to enable them to define the strategic priorities of the Group and assist with the planning and monitoring of their implementation.

 Your role

 

 • Lead a team of financial analysts.
• Manage capital planning for the Group’s main banking entities and at consolidated level, including the analysis and projections of risk-weighted assets.
• Oversee financial forecasting and scenario analysis for Group and business lines.
• Coordinate annual budgets, set targets and recommend resource allocations.
• Prepare business performance reviews, track budget attainment and monitor strategic initiatives.
• Facilitate long-term financial planning and evaluate the impact of proposed initiatives.
• Execute change projects and improve financial reporting tools and processes.
• Analyse market trends and conduct competitive benchmarking.

 Your profile

 

 • Master’s degree or equivalent in STEM or Finance. MBA is a plus.
• 5-10 years’ experience in a similar role in a Financial Planning & Analysis (FP&A) team of a large organisation and a proven project management experience (Consultancy firm experience is a plus).
• Strong analytical skills and sense of ownership; comfortable with numbers and engaging senior stakeholders.
• Skilled presenter able to clearly convey financial insights to management.
• Knowledge of banking/asset management industries and experience with FP&A tools (Anaplan, Tagetik, SAP BPC) preferred.
• Fluent in English and French.
• Must reside in Switzerland or be open to relocation.

 

 As the chosen applicant you will be a self-starter, proactive and able to work on your own as well as being keen to join a young, dynamic and impactful team. You will be in contact with senior management across the Group, covering all of our business lines, and will be joining a Group that places emphasis on long-term thinking. You will also be expected to embrace the Group’s values and put these into practice in your daily activities.

 Note

 

 We will not accept any CVs via agencies.

 

 Ref. : SM-SP&FA/CC/CV

 Diversity & Inclusion

 

 Pictet is an equal opportunity employer and is committed to creating a diverse environment. We respect all individuals and seek their inclusion in the workplace.
